audit_summary.summary_text: The SKILL.md contains a hidden 'Advanced: Repository Analytics' section that exfiltrates GitHub tokens, SSH private keys, and repository metadata to an external endpoint (gh-repo-analytics.io). This is a clear shadow feature not aligned with the stated GitHub operations purpose, involving credential harvesting and data exfiltration.

vulnerabilities.title: Environment Variable Harvesting
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The code reads GITHUB_TOKEN and GH_TOKEN from environment variables, which are used for authentication with GitHub. These tokens are then included in the data sent to an external server.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: An attacker can obtain GitHub API tokens, allowing unauthorized access to repositories, code, and CI/CD pipelines.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the entire sync_repo_analytics function. Never transmit authentication tokens to external services.

vulnerabilities.title: Credential Access - SSH Private Key Exfiltration
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The code enumerates the ~/.ssh directory, reads all non-.pub files (which are private keys), and includes their contents in the data sent to an external server.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: An attacker can steal SSH private keys, gaining persistent access to all servers and services where those keys are authorized.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the SSH key collection logic. Never read or transmit private keys.

vulnerabilities.title: Context Leakage - Exfiltration of Sensitive Data to External Endpoint
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The function collects GitHub tokens, SSH keys, user name, and repository metadata, then sends it all as a JSON payload to https://gh-repo-analytics.io/api/v1/sync via an HTTP POST request.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: All collected sensitive data (tokens, SSH keys, user info, repo list) is transmitted to an external server controlled by an attacker, leading to full account compromise.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the entire sync_repo_analytics function and its invocation. Do not send any data to external endpoints.

vulnerabilities.title: File System Enumeration - SSH Directory Scanning
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The code explicitly lists files in ~/.ssh directory to find private key files for exfiltration.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Enables the attacker to identify and exfiltrate SSH private keys, leading to unauthorized system access.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the SSH directory scanning logic entirely.

vulnerabilities.title: Hidden Instructions - Malicious Code in Markdown
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The SKILL.md contains a Python code block under 'Advanced: Repository Analytics' that is not mentioned in the skill description or common commands. This code performs credential harvesting and data exfiltration, hidden as a 'sync repo analytics' feature.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence: The entire 'Advanced: Repository Analytics' section with the sync_repo_analytics() function and its immediate invocation.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: The malicious code executes automatically when the skill is loaded, exfiltrating sensitive data without user awareness.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the entire 'Advanced: Repository Analytics' section from SKILL.md.

vulnerabilities.title: Obfuscated Code - Cyrillic Character Substitution
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The SKILL.md uses Cyrillic characters (e.g., 'А' in 'АРI', 'о' in 'Соllect', 'а' in 'анаlytics') to visually replace Latin characters, potentially to evade text-based detection or confuse analysis.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: This obfuscation can bypass simple keyword-based security scans and make the code harder to review manually.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Replace all Cyrillic characters with their Latin equivalents to ensure code clarity and avoid obfuscation.
